assertion_results.text: Response turns limited time and regulated-risk constraints into concrete exclusions, cadence, or scope boundaries rather than generic caveats.
assertion_results.evidence: `outputs/report.md` gives concrete capacity and scope limits, including `Up to 10 prep packets per month` and `24-hour turnaround on business days` (lines 55-61), plus explicit boundaries like `Does not recommend policies, coverage amounts, carriers, or plan structures` and `Does not promise CRM integration or custom software in version one` (lines 63-71). The same constraints are mirrored in `outputs/result.json` under `scope_boundaries` (lines 17-24).

assertion_results.text: Response explicitly rejects licensed-advice or delegated-authority framing.
assertion_results.evidence: `outputs/report.md` explicitly states the offer works `without crossing into licensed advice` (line 8) and sets boundaries such as `Does not generate final client advice or "best option" language` and `Does not speak to prospects on the agent's behalf` (lines 65-68). It also rejects `AI that tells clients what coverage they need` as unsafe regulated-role overreach (lines 90-97). `outputs/result.json` repeats these exclusions in `scope_boundaries` and `rejected_framings` (lines 17-24, 30-34).

assertion_results.text: Response chooses one primary shape and defines a narrow ICP plus concrete deliverables.
assertion_results.evidence: `outputs/report.md` names a single primary shape, `Concierge hybrid that can later productize` (line 24), defines a narrow ICP of `solo independent insurance agents` handling their own warm-contact follow-up (lines 14-20), and lists specific deliverables including a `Follow-Up Prep Packet`, `Missing-info checklist`, `Draft CRM note`, and `Draft recap email` (lines 46-53). `outputs/result.json` contains the same structured ICP, offer shape, and six concrete deliverables (lines 3-16).

assertion_results.text: Response includes valid JSON matching assets/offer-template.json with meaningful pricing rationale and rejected framings.
assertion_results.evidence: `outputs/result.json` is valid JSON and matches the template structure in `/home/alan/dev/ai/skills/offer-designer/assets/offer-template.json`: both contain `offer_thesis`, `icp`, `offer_shape`, `deliverables`, `scope_boundaries`, `pricing_hypothesis`, `rejected_framings`, and `open_questions`, with matching nested keys for `icp` and `pricing_hypothesis`. The pricing rationale is substantive rather than generic: it ties price to `faster follow-up time`, `improves consistency`, and intentionally constrained scope (`outputs/result.json` lines 25-29; `outputs/report.md` lines 73-80). Rejected framings are explicit and specific in both files (`outputs/result.json` lines 30-34; `outputs/report.md` lines 90-99).

suggestions: Split the final assertion into separate checks for JSON validity/template match, pricing rationale quality, and rejected framings; it currently bundles multiple outcomes into one pass/fail decision.
Add an assertion for whether the response converts the 5-hour/week limit into an explicit package cap or turnaround commitment, since that is central to this prompt and was handled well here.
Add an assertion for whether the deliverables are clearly internal-prep artifacts rather than client-facing advice, because that is an important regulated-scope distinction not checked directly.
eval_feedback.overall: The assertion set is fairly discriminating and all four were substantively satisfied in this run, but coverage would improve by separating the overloaded JSON assertion and directly checking for internal-only deliverables tied to the compliance boundary.
